key_structures.name: scan_state_dir_for_cleanup
key_structures.signature: (dir: &Path, state: &str, failed_age_days: u32, result: &mut ScanResult) -> anyhow::Result<()>

patterns_discovered.description: Scans root .cards/ directory, then iterates team-* subdirectories for each state, ensuring comprehensive coverage of card distribution
patterns_discovered.evidence: nested loop structure: states → team_dirs iteration with `starts_with('team-')` filter

patterns_discovered.description: Single pass identifies 4 distinct problem categories: corrupt metadata, age-based cleanup, orphaned processes, build artifact cleanup
patterns_discovered.evidence: ScanResult struct with 4 independent Vec fields populated by different conditional branches

patterns_discovered.description: Defensive approach with existence checks, safe metadata access, and graceful error handling throughout scanning
patterns_discovered.evidence: bop_core::read_meta().is_err(), fs::metadata().ok(), metadata.modified().ok() chains, unwrap_or_default() fallbacks

patterns_discovered.description: Team directories sorted to ensure consistent, reproducible scan results across invocations
Only processes cards with .bop or .jobcard extensions to skip unrelated files in state dirs

gotchas_discovered.description: Empty detection uses iterator pattern with .next().is_none() which is idiomatic Rust but somewhat obscure; could benefit from helper function
gotchas_discovered.location: lines ~89-94 for output/ and ~97-102 for logs/
gotchas_discovered.impact: Code correctness not affected, but readability could be improved for maintenance

gotchas_discovered.description: Orphan detection only checks if output/logs directories are empty, does not verify the PID is actually dead. A card could be running but have not yet written output
gotchas_discovered.impact: Could incorrectly mark active cards as orphaned if they have not yet produced output

gotchas_discovered.description: Age calculation uses unwrap_or_default() on duration_since() which could mask clock skew or system time changes; considers such cards as 0 seconds old
gotchas_discovered.impact: Edge case: if system clock moves backward, old failed cards might not be cleaned up

gotchas_discovered.description: No limit on number of team-* directories scanned per state; could be expensive on systems with many teams
gotchas_discovered.impact: Performance degrades linearly with team count; acceptable for typical deployments (< 100 teams) but could be optimized

gotchas_discovered.description: States are hardcoded as string array rather than derived from a central constant, creating maintenance risk if state machine evolves
gotchas_discovered.impact: If new state added to card model, this function must be manually updated

key_achievements: Implemented complete card scanning traversal of hierarchical directory structure (root + team dirs)
Identified 4 categories of problematic cards: corrupt metadata, age-expired failed cards, orphaned running processes, stale build artifacts
Covered all 6 card states: drafts, pending, running, done, failed, merged
Returned structured ScanResult for downstream cleanup operations
Applied defensive error handling throughout (is_err checks, option chains, graceful defaults)
approach_outcome.integration_readiness: High - function signature and result struct are clean, testable, and extensible for future cleanup logic

recommendations.recommendation: Implement actual PID verification for orphan detection using nix::sys::signal::kill(pid, None) or equivalent to check process liveness before marking as orphaned
recommendations.justification: Current empty-output check is false-positive prone; active cards might not have written to output yet

recommendations.recommendation: Extract hardcoded state list to a shared constant (e.g., const CARD_STATES: &[&str] = [...]) in bop_core to ensure consistency across codebase
recommendations.justification: Reduces coupling, single source of truth for card lifecycle

recommendations.recommendation: Add optional parameter to limit team directory scan depth or cached team list to avoid O(teams) enumeration per state if deployed at scale
recommendations.justification: Future-proofs for deployments with many team-* directories

recommendations.recommendation: Extract empty directory checks into helper function (e.g., is_dir_empty(path: &Path) -> bool) for clarity
recommendations.justification: Improves code readability and testability of edge case logic

recommendations.recommendation: Add debug logging at detection boundaries (e.g., 'Found N corrupt cards in root/.cards/failed/') to aid troubleshooting of stale card accumulation
recommendations.justification: Helps operators diagnose why cleanup is not working as expected

recommendations.recommendation: Add unit tests covering: missing state dirs, permission errors, symlinks in card paths, team dirs starting with 'team-' prefix edge cases
recommendations.justification: Ensures robustness in production scenarios (e.g., corrupted filesystem, race conditions)
